If you want to allow users to change the default selection when they click on the "New" radio button, you can update the `stockStatus` field initialization like this:

```typescript
this.myFormGroup = this.formBuilder.group({
  stockStatus: [null, Validators.required], // set default value as null
  // ... other form fields
});
```

By setting the default value as `null`, none of the radio buttons will be pre-selected by default. Users will be able to select either "New" or "Used" as per their preference.

However, if you still want to have a default value of "Used" and allow users to change the selection, you can modify the code for the radio buttons:

```html
<label>
  <input type="radio" value="new" [checked]="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').value === 'new'" (change)="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').setValue('new')"> New
</label>

<label>
  <input type="radio" value="used" [checked]="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').value === 'used'" (change)="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').setValue('used')"> Used
</label>
```

In the code snippet above, `[checked]="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').value === 'new'"` conditionally sets the `checked` attribute of the "New" radio button based on the current value of the `stockStatus` form control. Similarly, `[checked]="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').value === 'used'"` sets the `checked` attribute of the "Used" radio button.

`(change)="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').setValue('new')"` sets the value of the `stockStatus` form control to "new" when the "New" radio button is clicked. `(change)="myFormGroup.get('stockStatus').setValue('used')"` sets the value to "used" when the "Used" radio button is clicked.

This way, users can change the selection by clicking on the radio buttons, and the value of `stockStatus` in the form group will be updated accordingly.
